intro: Cienfuegosia australis, also known as the Firewheel flower, is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family, Malvaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cienfuegosia australis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Cienfuegosia australis include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
